_comment: PARAMETER METADATA — Single source of truth for all model parameters.
_usage: Every doc and script MUST reference parameter info from this file (by name). Do NOT duplicate tier/source/experiment info elsewhere.
_reference_paper: Mmeka, Dubarry, Bessler, J. Electrochem. Soc. 172 080538 (2025), DOI 10.1149/1945-7111/adf9cb

tiers.I: Read directly from datasheet or by convention (no fitting)
tiers.II: Measured directly and loaded as lookup tables (LUTs)
tiers.II-derived: Derived from II-level LUT at a canonical operating point
tiers.II-literature: Taken from published literature (e.g. XRD/dilatometry reference)
tiers.III: Simple scalar fit from 1-2 specific measurements (stable)
tiers.IV_calendar: Fitted from calendar-aging experiment (temperature x SOC x time)
tiers.IV_cycle: Fitted from cycle-aging experiment, pre-knee region
tiers.IV_knee: Fitted from cycle-aging experiment, full knee region

parameters.name: C_nominal_Ah
parameters.experiment: EXP-A: C/40 complete discharge on fresh cell
parameters.notes: Nominal capacity from datasheet or direct C/40 discharge integration. DISTINCT from C_PE^0 (which is the derived initial PE electrode capacity, see C0_PE entry).

parameters.name: C0_PE
parameters.experiment: Derived from EXP-A with correction factors; Appendix Eq. 51-54
parameters.notes: Initial PE ELECTRODE capacity (distinct from nominal cell capacity). Formula in code: C_nominal_Ah / dX_PE_alawa / (1-OFS/100) * 3600 / 0.973 * 1.0275. The '/0.973 * 1.0275' ~5.6% correction factors adjust simulated C/20 discharge vs experimental. Paper Appendix states C_PE^0 'is obtained by fitting a simulation against an experimental C/40 discharge curve' - effectively a scalar fit, not a direct measurement.

parameters.name: C0_NE
parameters.experiment: Derived from C0_PE via alawa electrode-balance formula
parameters.notes: Derived via Appendix Eq. 54: C_NE^0 = C_PE^0 * LR * dX_PE_alawa / dX_NE_alawa. Must be recomputed if any of {C0_PE, LR, dX_PE_alawa, dX_NE_alawa} changes.

parameters.name: Q0_SEI_NE
parameters.experiment: Derived from OFS via Appendix Eq. 55-56
parameters.notes: Initial SEI charge from cell formation. Formula: Q0_SEI_NE = C_PE^0 * dX_PE_alawa * OFS/100. Directly linked to OFS fit parameter.

parameters.name: X0_PE
parameters.experiment: Convention (full-discharge state of PE); can be cross-checked via half-cell data
parameters.notes: For NCA: at V_min the PE is near-saturated lithiated, X~0.95.

parameters.name: X0_NE
parameters.notes: For Graphite: at V_min the NE is near-depleted of Li, X~0.01.

parameters.name: dX_PE_alawa
parameters.experiment: From EXP-B1 half-cell data analysis (alawa convention)

paper_errata.description: Paper Table II reports 0.788 but MATLAB source code (Zenodo) uses 0.771. ~2.2% discrepancy, not acknowledged in paper text. Most likely cause: late-stage refit of half-cell data after paper text frozen. Impact on capacity prediction <2%. Code value is what actually produced Fig. 6-7.
paper_errata.action: Use 0.771 for reproducibility. Document in CRITICAL_REVIEW.md.

parameters.name: V_PE_0_curve
parameters.experiment: EXP-B1: Half-cell C/40 test on PE material (measured ONCE on fresh material)
parameters.notes: IMPORTANT: Only measured ONCE on fresh material. Never re-measured during aging - degradation effects enter via LAM_PE, not by updating this curve.

parameters.name: V_NE_0_curve
parameters.experiment: EXP-B2: Half-cell C/40 test on NE material (measured ONCE on fresh material)
parameters.notes: IMPORTANT: Only measured ONCE on fresh material.

parameters.name: R_s_LUT
parameters.experiment: EXP-B3: EIS high-frequency intercept on FRESH cell only
parameters.notes: CRITICAL: ONLY measured on fresh cell. R_s is assumed not to degrade (Eq. 46, f_R,s=1). Post-mortem literature (Stiaszny 2014) suggests this understates late-life behavior by 10-30%; see critical_review_findings.C3.

parameters.name: R_NE_LUT
parameters.experiment: EXP-B4: GITT + half-cell characterization on FRESH cell only
parameters.notes: CRITICAL: ONLY measured on fresh cell. Aging-induced resistance increase captured by time-varying factor f_R,NE (Eq. 45).

parameters.name: R_PE_LUT
parameters.experiment: EXP-B4: GITT + half-cell characterization on FRESH cell only
parameters.notes: CRITICAL: ONLY measured on fresh cell. Aging via f_R,PE = 1/(1-Q_LAM_PE/C0_PE), Eq. 44.

parameters.name: LR
parameters.notes: Paper explicitly notes this differs from Devie & Dubarry 2016 (LR=0.96) due to use of more recent half-cell data.

parameters.name: OFS
parameters.notes: Paper differs from Devie & Dubarry 2016 (OFS=2.8%) due to more recent half-cell data. OFS determines initial SEI capacity Q_SEI_NE^0.

parameters.name: C1
parameters.experiment: EXP-C: Current-step relaxation test on fresh cell

parameters.name: fractionR1toRs
parameters.notes: Paper states this was 'obtained via a constant factor by fitting to the dynamic DST test.' Despite the 0.5 value looking like a default, it is in fact a FIT RESULT for this specific cell. Name is historical (inherited from MATLAB original); the 'toRs' suffix does NOT imply a semantic relationship with R_s. Actual meaning: fraction of R_NE assigned to the static branch vs. dynamic (RC) branch. See 06_parameter_sourcing.md §3.3 for physical rationale and why horizontal (§2.2.3) and vertical (§3.3) resistance splits are both necessary.

parameters.name: fractionR2toRs
parameters.notes: Controls the static/dynamic split of R_PE (positive electrode). Name is historical (inherited from MATLAB original); the 'toRs' suffix does NOT imply a semantic relationship with R_s. Actual meaning: fraction of R_PE assigned to the static branch vs. dynamic (RC) branch. See 06_parameter_sourcing.md §3.3 for physical rationale. Paper value 0.5 is a FIT RESULT for Panasonic NCR18650B via FIT-3, not a hardcoded default.

parameters.name: k_SEI_cal
parameters.experiment: EXP-E: Calendar aging at multiple (T, SOC) over ~66 weeks

paper_errata.description: Paper Table Ib reports k_SEI_cal = 4.2e-22 A^2*s, but MATLAB Zenodo code has 0.0419625 ~= 4.2e-2 A^2*s. The factor-of-10^20 discrepancy is almost certainly a typesetting error (duplicated '2' in the exponent). Dimensional analysis of Eq. 36 plus forward-simulation sanity checks (a year of calendar aging at 50% SOC gives ~0.1 Ah LLI with the code value, matching Fig. 4c) confirm the code value is correct.
paper_errata.evidence: Dimensional analysis + MATLAB code + Fig. 4 reproduction.

parameters.name: E_a_SEI
parameters.notes: Consistent with Reniers 2019 SEI lineage. If only one temperature available, fix to 50000-60000 range.

paper_errata.description: In MATLAB source, Ea enters ONLY the SEI rate (Eq. 36). Plating, LAM_PE, LAM_NE have NO Arrhenius term. This limits temperature-extrapolation validity for those processes and is not explicitly acknowledged in the paper.
paper_errata.action: Document in scope_of_validity. Consider per-process Arrhenius for temperature extrapolation.

parameters.name: R_SEI
parameters.experiment: EXP-E (calendar aging, REQUIRES IR measurement at each RPT)
parameters.notes: CRITICAL: R_SEI fitted from CALENDAR aging data, NOT cycle. Paper p.12 verbatim: 'all resistance-related aging parameters were taken from the calendar degradation study.' Rationale: under calendar conditions Q_PLA_NE=0, so IR(t) growth comes purely from SEI+LAM, making R_SEI uniquely identifiable.

parameters.name: R_NE_0
parameters.experiment: Derived from R_NE_LUT at 50% SOC, C/3 discharge
parameters.notes: Reference scalar extracted from fresh-cell R_NE_LUT at canonical operating point. Must be RECOMPUTED whenever R_NE_LUT is replaced. Currently hand-coded; a derive_R_NE_0() utility should be added for new cell chemistries.

parameters.name: k_SEI_cyc
parameters.experiment: EXP-F: Cycle aging to ~150 EFC pre-knee

paper_errata.description: Paper p.10 text lists 'k_SEI,cyc' among CALENDAR degradation rate constants, contradicting Table Ib/Ic which correctly place k_SEI,cal in calendar and k_SEI,cyc in cycle. This is a narrative typo; Tables are correct.
paper_errata.action: JSON follows the Tables. Read page-10 text as 'k_SEI,cal' in calendar context.

parameters.name: k_LAM_NE_cal
parameters.notes: Paper assumes NE calendar LAM is negligible (fixed to 0).

parameters.name: k_LP
parameters.experiment: EXP-G: Cycle aging BEYOND knee (>400 EFC for NCA/G)
parameters.notes: ONLY free parameter in FIT-4c.

parameters.name: V_LP_eq
parameters.notes: See critical_review_findings.S2.

paper_errata.description: Static V_LP_eq=0V is deprecated by Beck-Greszta-Roberts-Dubarry 2024 (Batteries 10:408, same senior author): 'Typical testing and modeling assume that plating occurs at 0 V when measured against the charge carrier. While this might be true under thermodynamic equilibrium, this is not true outside of steady state.' Adequate for Baure-Dubarry 2019 dataset's tested conditions, but should be revised for fast-charge or low-temperature extrapolation.
paper_errata.action: TODO in code + scope-of-validity note. Implement dynamic form for extensions.

parameters.name: v_rel_PE_coeff
parameters.notes: 10-coefficient polynomial np.polyval-compatible. For LFP use near-constant (~6.8%).

parameters.name: v_rel_NE_coeff
parameters.notes: Graphite ~12% expansion, highly non-linear due to stage transitions. See critical_review_findings.C5.

parameters.name: X_LAM_PE
parameters.notes: Community-standard alawa convention.

FIT-0.name: Initial PE capacity correction
FIT-0.method: Manual or scalar fit of the 0.973/1.0275 correction factors
FIT-0.notes: Often skipped by using paper correction factors as-is. Re-derive only if cell differs substantially (>10%) from NCR18650B.

FIT-1.name: Electrode balancing
FIT-1.measurement_needed: V_cell(SOC) full-cell OCV and V_PE^0(X), V_NE^0(X) half-cell OCVs

FIT-2.name: RC transient fitting
FIT-2.measurement_needed: V(t) relaxation after current pulse termination at known SOC and pulse current
FIT-2.method: scipy.optimize.curve_fit two-exponential V(t)=V_inf+A1*exp(-t/tau1)+A2*exp(-t/tau2), then C_i = tau_i / R_i via R_NE/R_PE LUT lookup at (C_rate=I_pre/C_nominal, X_NE/X_PE).
FIT-2.tau_to_R_mapping: Two candidates A: tau1->R_NE+tau2->R_PE; B: tau2->R_NE+tau1->R_PE. Selection by amplitude RSS against expected -I_pre*R_NE/-I_pre*R_PE. Both candidates persisted to fit_report. See PARAMETER_SOP.md §三.2.
preconditions: material_specs/<cell>.material.json LR/OFS fitted (FIT-1 完成) so X_NE/X_PE map is consistent
param_specs/<cell>__mmeka2025.params.json resistance_mat 指向有效 .mat 文件
FIT-2.upgrade_path: 见 docs/CRITICAL_REVIEW.md C7 与 docs/UPGRADE_LITERATURE/fractional_order_RC.md。当 RMSE 持续超阈或残差呈现系统性结构, 走分数阶 RC / Mittag-Leffler 升级路径。

FIT-3.name: Resistance distribution
FIT-3.measurement_needed: Dynamic voltage response on fresh cell
FIT-3.known_limitation: RMSE in 30-50 mV range is a known limit of the two-RC topology (paper Fig. 3d shows a ~250 s voltage step the RC ladder cannot follow). Do not over-fit below this floor. See CRITICAL_REVIEW.md.

FIT-4a.name: Calendar aging parameters (INCLUDING R_SEI)
FIT-4a.measurement_needed: Capacity(t), IR(t), LLI(t), LAM_PE(t) at multiple (T, SOC)
FIT-4a.critical_constraint: IR(t) MUST be measured at each RPT to identify R_SEI. Paper p.12 confirms all resistance-related aging params come from calendar fit.
FIT-4a.skip_if_no_data: Set R_SEI to paper value (0.66), k_SEI_cal to paper default. Model loses quantitative IR prediction accuracy.

FIT-4b.name: Cycle aging parameters (pre-knee)
FIT-4b.measurement_needed: LLI(EFC), LAM_PE(EFC), LAM_NE(EFC) from IC analysis at RPTs

EXP-A.name: Nominal capacity & full-cell OCV
EXP-A.description: C/40 complete discharge on fresh cell from V_max to V_min

EXP-B1.name: PE half-cell OCV
EXP-B1.description: Disassemble cell, build CR2032 PE half-cell vs Li metal, C/40 voltage sweep

EXP-B2.name: NE half-cell OCV
EXP-B2.description: Same as B1 but for NE material

EXP-B3.name: Series resistance (EIS)
EXP-B3.description: EIS at 1kHz-10kHz on FRESH cell; high-frequency intercept = R_s

EXP-B4.name: Electrode resistance map (GITT)
EXP-B4.description: GITT on fresh cell with multiple SOC x C-rate pulses

protocol_details.splits: Use half-cell GITT if available; else EIS Nyquist fitting

EXP-C.name: RC transient
EXP-C.description: Current step -> rest -> relax; fit two-exponential decay

EXP-D.name: Dynamic protocol validation
EXP-D.description: First DST cycle on fresh cell, full voltage trace

EXP-E.name: Calendar aging
EXP-E.description: Multi-(T, SOC) long-term storage with periodic RPT
EXP-E.CRITICAL: Each RPT MUST include: (1) C/5 capacity, (2) IR pulse or EIS for internal resistance, (3) C/40 OCV for IC analysis -> LLI, LAM_PE, LAM_NE. Additionally: all RPTs must be performed at 25 ± 1 degC with ≥4 h thermal equilibration after removal from storage; channel/fixture assignment must be fixed per cell across the entire aging campaign; BOL/EOL reference-cell calibration and a sentinel cell are required for drift correction. See PARAMETER_SOP.md §二.4 and §二.5 for full protocol.

EXP-F.name: Cycle aging (pre-knee)
EXP-F.description: Continuous cycling (DST or user protocol) to ~150-200 EFC
EXP-F.RPT_requirements: Same RPT protocol as EXP-E. IR measurement is recommended but not strictly required for FIT-4b (R_SEI not re-fitted from cycle data, per rule R2). Temperature control (25 ± 1 degC) and device hygiene requirements are mandatory. See PARAMETER_SOP.md §二.

EXP-G.name: Cycle aging (through knee)
EXP-G.description: Continue EXP-F until capacity drops to ~70%
EXP-G.RPT_requirements: Same RPT protocol as EXP-E. IR measurement is recommended but not strictly required for FIT-4b (R_SEI not re-fitted from cycle data, per rule R2). Temperature control (25 ± 1 degC) and device hygiene requirements are mandatory. See PARAMETER_SOP.md §二.

deferred_extensions._comment: Architectural extensions evaluated in web-chat discussions and explicitly deferred. Do NOT begin implementation without a new task card from web chat.

R_temperature_dependence.summary: Add temperature dependence to R_s, R_NE, R_PE LUTs to enable cross-temperature performance prediction.
candidate_paths: A: Arrhenius shell on existing 2D LUTs (lightest, requires E_a calibration at 2-3 T points)
B: 3D LUT R(I, X, T) (medium cost, requires full B3+B4 at 3-5 T points)
C: Replace LUT with electrochemical R model (heavy, departs from ECM philosophy)
R_temperature_dependence.blocking: Required before any cross-temperature energy/power prediction.
R_temperature_dependence.next_step: New web-chat task card needed before implementation.

per_process_Arrhenius.summary: Add Arrhenius temperature dependence to plating, LAM_PE, LAM_NE rate constants. Currently only SEI has E_a.
per_process_Arrhenius.blocking: Required for cross-temperature aging-rate prediction. Independent of R(T) extension.
per_process_Arrhenius.experimental_cost: Multi-T versions of EXP-E, EXP-F, EXP-G; ~14 aging parameters total (up from 10).
per_process_Arrhenius.next_step: New web-chat task card needed before implementation.

ic_analysis_multi_rpt_smoothing.summary: Joint inversion across all RPTs of a cell with monotonicity / smoothness priors on (LLI, LAM_PE, LAM_NE) trajectories.
ic_analysis_multi_rpt_smoothing.blocking: Not blocking. v1 single-RPT inversion is sufficient for FIT-4a/b. Revisit only if v1 noise floor proves limiting.
ic_analysis_multi_rpt_smoothing.next_step: Revisit after v1 IC analysis (SPEC_ic_analysis.md) is in production use.

scope_of_validity._comment: Explicit scope limits for the published parameter set. Violating these requires re-fitting and/or model upgrades.
scope_of_validity.cell_type: Panasonic NCR 18650B, NCA cathode / Graphite anode, 3.35 Ah nominal
scope_of_validity.C_rate_range: Tested <= 1C in cycling, up to 1.6A (~0.5C) charge
scope_of_validity.plating_regime: Irreversible-plating regime only; Baure-Dubarry 2019 observed NO lithium stripping for this cell under DST
scope_of_validity.upper_cutoff_voltage: <= 4.2 V (higher voltages likely invalidate LLI_PE=0 assumption)
extrapolation_requires_upgrades: Low-temperature (<15degC) -> dynamic plating onset AND per-process Arrhenius
Large-format cells -> add thermal coupling (isothermal assumption breaks)

critical_review_findings._comment: Results of independent critical review 2026-04-20; full write-up in docs/CRITICAL_REVIEW.md

E1_k_SEI_cal_typo.summary: Table Ib value 4.2e-22 A^2s is a typesetting error; correct value is 4.2e-2 A^2s (= 0.0419625 in code).
E1_k_SEI_cal_typo.evidence: Dimensional analysis of Eq. 36 + MATLAB Zenodo source + forward-simulation sanity check.
E1_k_SEI_cal_typo.action: Code already uses correct value. JSON notes errata.

E2_page10_text_typo.summary: Paper p.10 narrative lists 'k_SEI,cyc' among CALENDAR degradation constants, but Tables clearly place it in cycle branch.
E2_page10_text_typo.evidence: Self-contradiction with Table Ib and Table Ic.

S1_dX_PE_alawa_mismatch.summary: Paper Table II dX_PE_alawa = 0.788 vs MATLAB 0.771 (2.2% diff).

S2_V_LP_eq_static.summary: Static V_LP_eq=0V is deprecated by Beck-Greszta-Roberts-Dubarry 2024. Inadequate for fast-charge or low-temperature extrapolation.
S2_V_LP_eq_static.action: TODO in code + scope-of-validity note in docs. Implement dynamic form for extensions.

S3_partial_Arrhenius.summary: Only SEI has Arrhenius. Plating, LAM_PE, LAM_NE have NO explicit temperature dependence. Limits temperature extrapolation.

C1_isothermal_assumption.summary: DST on 18650 can cause 5-10 K transient heating; at Ea=55.5kJ/mol, 10K raises rates by ~1.8x.
C1_isothermal_assumption.action: Document; thermal coupling extension needed for large-format cells.

C2_max0_BV_rectifier.severity: MODERATE general / LOW for this cell
C2_max0_BV_rectifier.summary: Irreversible-only plating via max(0,BV) is empirically justified for NCR 18650B (Baure-Dubarry 2019 observed no stripping).
C2_max0_BV_rectifier.evidence: O'Kane 2022 PCCP implements bidirectional; Baure-Dubarry 2019 justifies irreversible-only.

C3_R_s_nondegrading.summary: Assuming R_s doesn't degrade under-predicts late-life polarization by 10-30%. Relevant to knee timing.

C4_LLI_PE_zero.summary: Zero PE-side LLI (no CEI on NCA) is reasonable mid-life but breaks at high V, high T, or EOL.

C5_molar_volume_proxy_limits.summary: |dv/dX| captures diffusion-induced stress but misses rate effects, phase transitions, fatigue.

C6_knee_mechanism_scope.summary: Plating-positive-feedback is one of >=6 knee pathways in Attia 2022 framework.
C6_knee_mechanism_scope.action: Do not claim universal knee explanation.

C7_RC_topology_inadequacy_for_long_relax.severity: MODERATE for long pulses / low temperature, LOW for short pulses near room T
C7_RC_topology_inadequacy_for_long_relax.summary: Two-RC topology (论文 Fig. 3 与 FIT-2 模型) 用两个集中时间常数 tau1, tau2 描述电极弛豫, 但实测在 >100 s 量级的弛豫窗口中, 残差常呈系统性结构 (慢段拖尾、固相扩散主导项无法被两个指数充分捕获)。FIT-3 已在 PARAMETERS.json::fit_steps::FIT-3::known_limitation 注明 RMSE 30-50 mV 的下限属此源。强行用更多 RC 串扩并不解决根本问题, 且引入识别性恶化。
C7_RC_topology_inadequacy_for_long_relax.evidence: FIT-2 实测残差结构 + 论文 Fig. 3d ~250s 电压阶跃 RC ladder 跟不上的现象 + 文献中分数阶 ECM / Mittag-Leffler 弛豫 / DRT 已被多次提议为 long-tail relaxation 的更适配描述。
C7_RC_topology_inadequacy_for_long_relax.action: 短期 (v0.5.x): 在 FIT2-W001 / FIT2-E003 中暴露残差结构, 让 marginal 与 fail 档明确指向本条。中长期 (v0.6+): 评估分数阶 RC (Constant Phase Element CPE) 与 Mittag-Leffler 弛豫升级路径, 见 docs/UPGRADE_LITERATURE/fractional_order_RC.md。relaxation_fitting.py 已为升级预留 RELAXATION_MODELS 调度入口。

C_X_LAM_convention.summary: X_LAM=0 (fully delithiated lost AM) is community-standard alawa convention.

N1_LR_OFS_collinearity.summary: LR 和 OFS 在 V_cell(SOC) 数据上存在强共线性。OFS 仅通过 (1-OFS/100) 因子影响 X_PE 的 SOC 范围, 这可被 LR 对 X_NE 范围的调整反向补偿。OFS 的独立可识别性依赖半电池 OCV 在特定 X 值附近的局部特征 (stage transitions, dV/dX 极值), 要求 EXP-A 数据 SOC 覆盖到这些特征点。
N1_LR_OFS_collinearity.evidence: v0.4.0 FIT-1 实施过程中, 100 点均匀采样的合成数据上, LR 反演相对误差 0.04%, OFS 反演相对误差 3.8%。真实 EXP-A 数据可能因为 stage transition 附近自然采样更密而表现更好, 但未验证。
N1_LR_OFS_collinearity.action: 记录现象。FIT-1 工作流上, 若 OFS uncertainty 偏大, 考虑 --fix-OFS 选项 (待 v0.4.x 实施) 把 OFS 固定到 datasheet 或 alawa 默认值, 只拟合 LR。

external_references.paper: Mmeka, Dubarry, Bessler (2025), J. Electrochem. Soc. 172:080538, DOI 10.1149/1945-7111/adf9cb
alawa_foundation: Dubarry, Truchot, Liaw (2012), J. Power Sources 219:204
external_references.calendar_aging_basis: Reniers, Mulder, Howey (2019), J. Electrochem. Soc. 166:A3189
external_references.knee_review: Attia et al. (2022), J. Electrochem. Soc. 169:060517
external_references.plating_snowball_origin: Yang et al. (2017), J. Power Sources 360:28
external_references.plating_bidirectional: O'Kane et al. (2022), Phys. Chem. Chem. Phys. 24:7909
external_references.molar_volume_source: Lin et al. (2013), J. Electrochem. Soc. 160:A1701
